models.generation_context: 3rd-gen IS (XE30), introduced 2014, mid-cycle refreshed in 2021 and again for 2026. The 2026 refresh restyles the instrument panel and center console, adds a new 12.3-inch Lexus Interface touchscreen and 12.3-inch digital cluster, and consolidates the lineup to a single IS 350 model (the IS 300 and the V8 IS 500 were both discontinued after MY2025).
models.model_summary: Compact rear-drive sport sedan with a 3.5L V6, offered for 2026 only as the IS 350 in F SPORT Design and F SPORT grades, each in RWD or AWD. The V8 IS 500 F SPORT Performance has been retired, so the 2026 IS is no longer offered in a V8 performance variant. A Special Appearance Package limited to 350 units adds matte-white paint and unique interior treatment.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 MY-specific VDS for the IS (VDS measures 3-year-old cars). The current-gen IS platform and 2GR-FKS 3.5L V6 are mature designs with a strong long-term reliability history; Consumer Reports has historically rated the IS above average for predicted reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Note: the Lexus IS was named the TOP OVERALL MODEL in JD Power's 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study, which strongly correlates with high owner-appeal scores. Owner sentiment historically positive on driving feel, build quality, and interior materials. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers credit the refreshed 2026 IS with a much-improved infotainment system and cleaner cabin design, retaining the chassis's well-loved tactile steering feel and balance. Common critiques are that the platform itself is now over a decade old, cargo space and rear-seat room trail newer rivals, and the discontinuation of the V8 IS 500 removes the most distinctive variant from the lineup.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB owner-review sample sizes for 2026 MY are not yet meaningful this early in the model year. 2024-2025 IS owners rate the car highly for build, driving feel, and reliability; common complaints are cramped rear seat, small trunk, and (prior to 2026) dated infotainment.

notable_other: new 12.3-inch touchscreen and 12.3-inch digital cluster (new for 2026)
F SPORT Design styling with 19-inch wheels and unique mesh grille

trims.notes: Base trim of the IS for 2026 (RWD ICE line). NHTSA and IIHS have not yet rated the refreshed 2026 IS; carryover 2025 IS held a 5-star NHTSA overall and IIHS-published frontal/side ratings but not a TSP award. 0-60 of 5.6s is Lexus's published figure for the IS 350 RWD. Manufacturer primary source www.lexus.com returned thin content to WebFetch; spec values cross-validated against pressroom.lexus.com and cars.com during research.

changes: Substitutes 6-speed automatic for the RWD model's 8-speed Sport Direct Shift unit
Highway MPG drops 1 mpg (26 vs 27) on the EPA cycle
Slightly heavier curb weight; all other specs carry over

trims.notes: AWD variant of the F SPORT Design. Shares all body/interior with the RWD F SPORT Design. AWD configurations use a 6-speed automatic; RWD configurations use an 8-speed. Curb weight increases ~150 lb for AWD (Lexus does not publish a separate spec).

changes: Upgrades to Mark Levinson 17-speaker premium audio (vs 10-speaker)
Adds ventilated front seats and Head-Up Display
Adds available rear spoiler and optional red brake calipers (new for 2026)

trims.notes: F SPORT (RWD) adds the chassis hardware (AVS, LSD) and premium audio over F SPORT Design. Shares dimensions/EPA figures with F SPORT Design RWD. The Special Appearance Package (350 units, Hakugin Matte White) is an option pack on this trim and is not separately broken out here per the master spec rule on optional packages.

changes: Adds Adaptive Variable Suspension (AVS) and Mark Levinson 17-speaker audio (F SPORT content)
Adds ventilated front seats and Head-Up Display
Highway MPG drops 1 mpg vs RWD
Loses the Torsen rear LSD (Lexus offers AVS but not the LSD on AWD IS)

trims.notes: Combined AWD + full F SPORT hardware. Note: rear LSD is not available on AWD configurations of the IS 350; AVS still standard. The Special Appearance Package is offered on F SPORT trims; not broken out as separate trim.

models.notes: IS 500 V8 was retired after MY2025 — not in the 2026 lineup. Lineup consolidates to IS 350 only. Trim_family groupings: F SPORT Design (RWD+AWD share styling and equipment except drivetrain), F SPORT (RWD+AWD share styling, AVS, audio). NHTSA has not yet posted 2026 IS ratings; carryover ratings likely apply. Lexus.com consumer page returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used for primary citations.

models.generation_context: 8th-gen ES, all-new for 2026 with battery-electric and hybrid powertrains (no gas-only ES for 2026). Built on the e-TNGA-derived platform with a 3.1-inch-longer wheelbase, 2.2 in wider, 6.5 in longer, and 4.5 in taller than the outgoing 7th-gen ES.
models.model_summary: Midsize luxury sedan, redesigned for 2026 and offered in three powertrain lines: ES 350h hybrid (FWD/AWD), ES 350e single-motor EV (FWD), and ES 500e dual-motor EV (AWD). The 7th-gen ES 250/ES 300h/ES 350 are no longer offered. Adopts the NACS charging port on EV variants.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) named Lexus the highest-ranked brand overall (premium AND industry-wide) for the fourth consecutive year at 151 PP100 (industry avg = 204). The ES was not a 2026 model-level award winner (those went to IS, UX, GX), but the brand-wide reliability score applies. Consumer Reports 2026 placed Lexus #2 in reliability-only ranking with a brand score of 65. The 8th-gen ES is brand-new for 2026 and CR has not published a model-specific predicted-reliability score yet; the new BEV powertrains and redesigned cabin introduce more uncertainty than a typical Lexus redesign.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The 8th-gen ES is brand-new with no APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviews highlight the more spacious cabin, the unusual decision to sell the ES as either a hybrid or a BEV (and to drop the gas-only version), and the smooth ride characteristic of the ES line. Critiques focus on the polarizing 'unified spindle' grille treatment, the loss of the proven ES 350 V6, and the modest 272-307 mile EV range relative to newer EV sedans.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews not yet meaningful — production launch in early 2026. Carryover 7th-gen ES owners rate the previous car highly for comfort and quietness; complaints centered on dated infotainment and rear-seat headroom, both of which the 8th-gen ES addresses.

trims.notes: Base trim of the ES hybrid powertrain line (FWD). Hybrid system output 244 hp combined. Dimensions are approximations from press materials; final EPA fuel-tank, curb-weight and trunk figures not yet published. fueleconomy.gov does not yet have a 2026 ES 350h-specific entry as of 2026-05-12; source set to the 2026 Lexus model-browse page until EPA publishes. MPG values per Lexus manufacturer claim with EPA confirmation pending. ES 350h Premium AWD adds AWD for ~$1,400. Powertrain primary source on lexus.com consumer page returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used as the canonical primary.

changes: Adds AWD (rear motor adds torque on demand)
EPA city MPG drops to 46 (from 48), highway 42 (from 44), combined 44 (from 46)
All other equipment carries over from ES 350h Premium

trims.notes: AWD via Lexus's electric AWD (E-Four-style on-demand rear motor). MPG values per Lexus manufacturer claim; EPA-specific 2026 ES 350h entry pending.

changes: Upgrades to Mark Levinson 17-speaker premium audio (vs 10-speaker)
Upgrades to leather seating (vs synthetic leather)

trims.notes: Higher-spec hybrid trim. AWD version of Premium+ is also offered at $57,295 (not separately broken out beyond family).

changes: Adds Mark Levinson 17-speaker audio, panoramic sunroof, ventilated front seats, HUD, leather seating (Premium+ content)

trims.notes: Top hybrid trim. EPA-specific entry pending.

notable_other: NACS charging port standard (CCS adapter included)

trims.notes: Base trim of the ES BEV (ES 350e) powertrain line. Single-motor FWD, 74.7 kWh battery, 307 mi EPA range with standard 19-inch wheels (292 mi with optional 21-inch). NACS port standard; CCS adapter included. fueleconomy.gov does not yet show a specific 2026 ES 350e entry as of 2026-05-12 (the ES BEV launched March 2026); MPGe to be populated when EPA publishes — source set to model-browse page until then. Manufacturer-published range and 0-60. Lexus.com consumer page returned thin content to WebFetch; spec values cross-validated against pressroom.lexus.com.

notable_other: 21-inch wheels (reduces range to 292 mi from 307 mi on 19s)

changes: Adds ventilated front seats and Head-Up Display
21-inch wheels (reduces EPA range to 292 mi from 307 mi)

trims.notes: Top BEV ES trim. Range reduction from 307 to 292 mi reflects the heavier and more aero-disruptive 21-inch wheels.

Base trim of the ES 500e (dual-motor AWD BEV) powertrain line. Adds rear 118-hp motor over the 350e's single front motor, for 338 hp combined. EPA-specific 2026 ES 500e entry not yet published; MPGe pending. Range 276 mi (19s) / 272 (21s) per Lexus.

changes: Adds Mark Levinson 17-speaker, panoramic sunroof, ventilated seats, HUD, leather (Luxury content)

trims.notes: Top BEV ES trim with dual-motor AWD.

models.notes: ES is brand-new 8th-gen for 2026 with three powertrain lines: ES 350h hybrid (FWD/AWD), ES 350e single-motor FWD BEV, ES 500e dual-motor AWD BEV. NO gas-only ES variant for 2026 (the previous ES 350 V6 and ES 250 are gone). Sole-trim atomic rule applies to each powertrain line's lowest-priced trim (Premium FWD on 350h, Premium FWD on 350e, Premium AWD on 500e). EPA has not yet published 2026 ES entries on fueleconomy.gov as of 2026-05-12 (the model launched March 2026); MPG/MPGe values are manufacturer claims, source set to model-browse page. Dimensions are press-material approximations. Manufacturer primary source (www.lexus.com) returned thin content to WebFetch; spec values cross-validated against pressroom.lexus.com which is the canonical primary citation.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen LS (XF50), introduced 2018, in its final model year. 2026 is offered exclusively as the LS 500 AWD Heritage Edition — a single-spec farewell limited to 250 US units. The LS 500h hybrid is no longer offered.
models.model_summary: Lexus's full-size flagship sedan in its final model year, offered only as a single-spec LS 500 AWD Heritage Edition (250 units) with a 3.4L twin-turbo V6 making 416 hp. The hybrid LS 500h has been retired. Production ends after MY2026.

reliability.summary: 5th-gen LS has historically rated above average for predicted reliability; no 2026 MY-specific data published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The LS is low-volume in its final-year Heritage Edition phase and may not receive a model-specific APEAL ranking. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Heritage Edition's send-off character and continued cabin opulence while observing that the 5th-gen LS has fallen behind newer flagship rivals on infotainment, autonomous-driving aids, and rear-seat tech. Common praise: ride quality, build, and the Mark Levinson 23-speaker audio. Common critique: the platform is dated and the lack of hybrid for 2026 narrows powertrain choice.

owner_reviews.summary: Sample sizes too small for 2026 MY. Outgoing LS owners on Edmunds/KBB praise serenity, build, and ride; common complaints are infotainment lag and tight trunk.

notable_other: unique exclusive exterior color and interior treatment

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (singleton powertrain line and singleton trim_family) — is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per the sole-trim atomic rule. Final-year LS, limited to 250 US units. LS 500h hybrid retired for 2026. Manufacturer primary source (www.lexus.com) returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used as canonical primary citation.

models.notes: Final model year for the LS sedan. 2026 lineup consists of a single Heritage Edition AWD spec (250 US units). LS 500h hybrid retired.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen LC (Z100), introduced 2018, available as coupe and convertible. 2026 is a carryover year with the LC 500 Convertible adding a limited Inspiration variant (350 NA units, Wind exterior color, Saddle Tan interior). LC 500h hybrid is not offered for 2026.
models.model_summary: Lexus's halo grand tourer, offered as the LC 500 coupe and LC 500 Convertible. Both are powered by a naturally aspirated 5.0L V8 (471 hp, 398 lb-ft) and a 10-speed automatic. The 2026 Convertible Inspiration is a limited-run appearance package; no hybrid LC for 2026.

reliability.summary: Low-volume halo car; small sample sizes constrain reliability data. Owner reports through 2024 indicate strong reliability, in line with Lexus norm.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Strong owner sentiment historically. The LC is low-volume and may not receive a model-specific APEAL ranking even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to praise the LC's exceptional ride/handling balance, the naturally aspirated V8 soundtrack, and one of the most beautifully detailed interiors at any price. Common critiques are tight rear seats (effectively 2+2), modest trunk, and a touch-pad infotainment input that has aged poorly.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are sparse; 2024-2025 carryover sentiment is strongly positive on driving experience and styling, with criticisms centered on the touch-pad UI and tight rear seats.

notable_other: 21-inch forged aluminum wheels (front and rear staggered)

trims.notes: Base/sole trim of the LC coupe family (singleton trim_family). LC 500h hybrid not offered for 2026. Manufacturer primary source (www.lexus.com) returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used as canonical primary.

notable_other: 21-inch forged aluminum wheels (front and rear staggered)

trims.notes: Sole trim of its own family (the Convertible is a distinct body style from the Coupe — separate trim_family, separate sole-trim). 2026 Convertible Inspiration is a limited 350-unit appearance package, not broken out as a separate trim per the master spec rule on optional packages.

models.notes: LC has two separate trim_family entries (coupe, convertible); each is a singleton family with a single trim, so both get is_base_trim:true and delta_from_base:null per the sole-trim atomic rule. The Convertible Inspiration limited edition is treated as an option package on the Convertible (per master spec §6.5).

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen NX (AZ20), introduced 2022, mid-cycle for 2026. The NX 250 is discontinued (the turbo NX 350 is the new ICE entry), the NX 350h gains a FWD option, the F SPORT Handling grade is now available on hybrid models, and a Premium grade is added to the PHEV line as a more affordable entry. PHEVs get dual-voltage charging cables (Level 1 and Level 2).
models.model_summary: Compact luxury crossover offered with three powertrains: NX 350 turbo gas, NX 350h hybrid (FWD or AWD, new for 2026), and NX 450h+ plug-in hybrid AWD. The NX 250 base gas trim has been dropped for 2026. Standard Lexus Safety System+ 3.0.

reliability.summary: Carryover 2nd-gen NX has rated above average in both JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports predicted reliability since launch. No 2026-MY-specific score published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Carryover 2024-2025 NX has rated well on APEAL. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the NX for its refined interior, the strong NX 450h+ PHEV powertrain (304 hp, 37-mile EV range), and standard Lexus Safety System+ 3.0. Common critiques: the turbo NX 350 sounds coarse under load, rear cargo trails some compact SUV rivals, and option pricing climbs quickly.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner samples not yet meaningful for 2026 MY. Carryover NX owners rate the car highly for build and comfort; common complaints center on the touchscreen size on lower trims and PHEV charging time on Level 1.

trims.notes: Base trim of the NX 350h hybrid powertrain line and new FWD configuration for 2026. IIHS TSP+ awarded to the 2026 NX. Manufacturer primary source (www.lexus.com) returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used as canonical primary citation; spec values cross-validated against secondary sources during research.
AWD variant of NX 350h. Lexus charges $1,550 for AWD across the NX hybrid range.
Base trim of the NX 350 ICE powertrain line (AWD only — the NX 350 is AWD-standard). IIHS TSP+ for 2026.

notable_other: F SPORT styling with mesh grille

changes: Adds Adaptive Variable Suspension and F SPORT chassis tuning
Adds 10-inch digital cluster, HUD, panoramic sunroof
Adds ventilated front seats, leather, heated steering wheel

trims.notes: Top NX 350 ICE trim — F SPORT Handling with AVS, larger screens, and Mark Levinson audio.

notable_other: NEW Premium grade lowers PHEV entry price for 2026

trims.notes: Base of the NX 450h+ PHEV powertrain line (new Premium grade for 2026 — lowers PHEV entry). 18.1 kWh battery, 37-mi EPA EV range. CCS1 port; dual-voltage cable standard. fueleconomy.gov did not return a specific 2026 NX 450h+ entry under the model-browse listing — source set to brand model-browse page until EPA publishes.

notable_other: F SPORT Handling NEWLY available on PHEV for 2026

changes: F SPORT Handling NEW on PHEV for 2026
Adds Adaptive Variable Suspension and F SPORT chassis tuning
Adds ventilated seats, leather, heated steering wheel

trims.notes: F SPORT Handling on the PHEV is new for 2026 — was previously only available on the ICE NX 350.

models.notes: Three powertrain lines (NX 350 ICE / NX 350h hybrid / NX 450h+ PHEV) each have a base trim per the multi-powertrain rule. NX 250 dropped for 2026 — turbo NX 350 is now the ICE entry. NX 350h FWD is new for 2026 (previously hybrid was AWD-only). PHEV gets a new Premium grade and dual-voltage cable standard. IIHS 2026 TSP+. Manufacturer primary source www.lexus.com returned thin content to WebFetch; pressroom.lexus.com used as canonical.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen RX (AL30), introduced 2023. 2026 carries over the lineup with wireless phone charging now standard across the range, the 450h+ PHEV gaining a Premium grade (lowering PHEV entry price), and Lexus Safety System+ 3.0 standard. The 3-row RX L was discontinued after the 4th gen and is not in the 5th-gen lineup.
models.model_summary: Midsize luxury crossover offered with four powertrains: RX 350 turbo gas (FWD/AWD), RX 350h hybrid (AWD), RX 450h+ plug-in hybrid AWD, and RX 500h F SPORT Performance hybrid AWD. 5-passenger 2-row (the 3-row RX L is not part of the 5th gen).

reliability.summary: Carryover 5th-gen RX has been one of JD Power's higher-ranked midsize luxury crossovers since launch. Consumer Reports predicted reliability historically above average. No 2026-MY-specific score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Carryover 5th-gen RX has historically scored well on APEAL. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the RX one of the most refined midsize luxury crossovers, especially in 350h hybrid and 500h F SPORT Performance forms. Common critiques: tight cargo for the class, occasionally laggy infotainment, and that the 350 turbo gas engine sounds coarse compared to the V6 it replaced.

owner_reviews.summary: 2024-2025 RX owner sentiment positive on ride/quietness; common complaints touch-pad/screen interaction and cargo space.

notable_other: Wireless phone charging now standard (new for 2026)

trims.notes: Base trim of the RX 350 ICE powertrain line. FWD; AWD optional at +$1,600.
AWD variant of the RX 350 base.

notable_other: Wireless phone charging now standard (new for 2026)

trims.notes: Base of the RX 350h hybrid powertrain line. AWD standard.

notable_other: NEW Premium grade for 2026 (lowers PHEV entry price)

trims.notes: Base trim of the RX 450h+ PHEV powertrain line. New Premium grade for 2026. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 RX 450h+ entry; source set to brand model-browse page.

changes: Luxury-grade interior trim and ambient lighting

trims.notes: Top RX 450h+ trim.

notable_other: DIRECT4 AWD with Dynamic Rear Steering

trims.notes: Sole-trim performance hybrid (singleton powertrain line and singleton trim_family — high-output 2.4T hybrid is distinct from the 2.5L Atkinson-cycle hybrid in RX 350h). Per sole-trim atomic rule: is_base_trim:true and delta_from_base:null.

models.notes: Four powertrain lines: RX 350 turbo gas, RX 350h Atkinson hybrid, RX 450h+ PHEV, RX 500h F SPORT Performance hybrid (high-output 2.4T). Each has its own base. RX 500h is treated as its own powertrain line because the engine, transmission (6AT vs CVT) and hybrid system differ from the RX 350h. RX L (3-row) is not part of the 5th gen — discontinued. IIHS rated TSP for 2025 (carryover for 2026).

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen GX (J250), introduced 2024 on the body-on-frame TNGA-F platform shared with Toyota Land Cruiser/4Runner/Tacoma. 2026 raises pricing $1,350 on most trims; lineup unchanged.
models.model_summary: Body-on-frame midsize luxury SUV with optional 3rd row, twin-turbo V6 (349 hp), full-time 4WD standard, and a strong towing capacity. Offered in six trims spanning on-road Luxury and off-road Overtrail orientations.

reliability.summary: 2nd-MY of the 3rd-gen platform; no JD Power 2026 VDS yet. The shared TNGA-F architecture has accumulated mixed initial-quality data across siblings (4Runner/Land Cruiser/Tacoma) with multiple recalls in 2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Note: the Lexus GX was a 2026 JD Power VDS Model Award winner, which strongly correlates with high APEAL ratings. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned GX for its genuine off-road capability, mid-decade-modern interior, and the strong twin-turbo V6. Common critiques: thirsty for the class (17 MPG combined), limited 3rd-row room, and ride quality on broken pavement.

owner_reviews.summary: Carryover sentiment positive on off-road capability and styling; complaints on fuel economy and cargo behind the 3rd row.

notable_other: Full-time 4WD with Torsen LSD center diff

trims.notes: Base trim of the GX. The GX_PRESS URL (pricing announcement) is hosted on a Lexus dealer site (carbuzz.com mirror) per the original press release; canonical primary is pressroom.lexus.com but Lexus has not published a single 2026 GX press release URL — using lexus.com/models/GX for the canonical citation.

changes: Adds ventilated front seats and heated rear seats
Adds wireless phone charger console upgrade

trims.notes: Step-up of the on-road Premium line.

changes: Adaptive Variable Suspension (AVS) for on-road comfort

trims.notes: Top on-road luxury trim.

changes: Adds Luxury+ exclusive interior trim and 2-tone seats
Mark Levinson audio, AVS, HUD (Luxury content)

trims.notes: Top of the on-road GX range — 6-seat captain's-chair layout.
Off-road-oriented Overtrail. 2-row 5-passenger only (no 3rd row).

changes: All Overtrail off-road hardware (lockers, MTS, E-KDSS, all-terrain tires)
Adds Mark Levinson audio, HUD, ventilated seats, premium leather
Roof rack and additional off-road accessories

trims.notes: Top of the Overtrail line.

models.notes: Single powertrain (3.4L twin-turbo V6); six trims grouped into three trim_families (on-road Premium tier, on-road Luxury tier, Overtrail off-road). Overtrail trims are 5-passenger 2-row only; the other four are 7-passenger 3-row (Luxury+ is 6-passenger with captain's chairs). 2026 raises most trim MSRPs by $1,350 vs 2025.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen LX (J310), introduced 2022 alongside the Toyota Land Cruiser 300. 2026 adds an F SPORT appearance package on the LX 700h Ultra Luxury and brings minor styling/trim updates.
models.model_summary: Full-size body-on-frame luxury SUV offered as the LX 600 (twin-turbo V6 gas) and LX 700h (hybrid). Standard 4WD with Torsen LSD center diff, 8,000 lb towing, 6-7 passenger configurations.

reliability.summary: Low-volume flagship; reliability data sparse. Carryover LX 600 has had multiple recalls related to the J310 platform.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The LX is low-volume and may not receive a model-specific APEAL ranking. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the LX 600/700h for serious off-road capability paired with luxury appointments and class-leading towing. Common critiques: weight (5,600+ lb), thirst, third-row tightness, and that the rival GLS-Class is more refined on-road.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews limited; complaints center on fuel economy and 3rd-row room, praise on capability and luxury.

notable_other: Full-time 4WD with Torsen LSD center diff

trims.notes: Base trim of LX 600 ICE powertrain line. Press release on pressroom.lexus.com primary citation.

notable_other: Adaptive Variable Suspension and Rear LSD

changes: F SPORT chassis hardware (AVS, Torsen rear LSD)
F SPORT styling, mesh grille, sport seats

trims.notes: F SPORT Handling LX 600 — 2-row 5-passenger only.
Top LX 600 luxury-oriented trim.
Base trim of the LX 700h hybrid powertrain line. Overtrail 5-passenger off-road orientation.

changes: Substitutes F SPORT chassis tuning (AVS) for Overtrail off-road hardware

trims.notes: On-road F SPORT alternative to the Overtrail.
Top 3-row luxury hybrid trim.

notable_other: NEW F SPORT appearance package available for 2026
4-passenger executive configuration with reclining rear captain's chairs

changes: 4-passenger executive layout with reclining rear captain's chairs
Refrigerated center console and power footrest
NEW F SPORT appearance package available for 2026

trims.notes: Top-of-range LX. The 2026 F SPORT appearance package adds black exterior trim, F SPORT badging, and mesh grille — appearance-only, no chassis changes (so still on Ultra Luxury chassis).

models.notes: Two powertrain lines: LX 600 (ICE) and LX 700h (hybrid), each with its own base. LX 600 base is Premium (7-passenger). LX 700h base is Overtrail (5-passenger off-road). F SPORT Handling and F SPORT (appearance) trims are 2-row 5-passenger; Luxury/Luxury+/Ultra Luxury are 3-row 7-passenger or 2-row 4-passenger executive. Pressroom.lexus.com primary citation for 2026 'Flagship SUV Returns' announcement.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen TX (introduced 2024 MY) on the TNGA-K unibody platform. Lexus's larger 3-row crossover, slotting above the RX. 2026 carryover.
models.model_summary: Midsize 3-row unibody crossover offered in TX 350 turbo gas (FWD/AWD), TX 500h F SPORT Performance hybrid AWD, and TX 550h+ plug-in hybrid AWD. 6 or 7 passengers depending on 2nd-row configuration.

reliability.summary: Newer model line (since 2024 MY); insufficient long-term data. Initial-quality data has been favorable.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The TX is in its 2nd MY of sales. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers credit the TX with a more usable 3rd row than the older RX L, a refined cabin, and a strong TX 500h F SPORT Performance variant. Common critiques: tight 3rd row vs Korean rivals (Telluride/Palisade), modest cargo behind the 3rd row, and the unfamiliar 6-passenger captain's-chair layout.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner samples small; positive on comfort and tech, critical of 3rd-row room.

trims.notes: Base trim of TX 350 ICE powertrain line. Manufacturer source www.lexus.com used for citations (canonical primary); content was thin to WebFetch — spec values cross-validated with cars.com during research.
AWD variant of TX 350.

notable_other: DIRECT4 AWD with Dynamic Rear Steering

trims.notes: Sole high-output hybrid trim (the TX 500h is offered in Premium and Luxury grades; here we treat the F SPORT Performance hybrid powertrain as its own line per the master spec's powertrain-line rule). Premium TX 500h MSRP starts around $70,000.

notable_other: 3.5L V6 PHEV with 18.1 kWh battery, 33-mi EV range

trims.notes: Sole-trim PHEV powertrain line (singleton powertrain line; per atomic rule is_base_trim:true and delta_from_base:null). Only TX with V6 engine. fueleconomy.gov has not yet published a specific 2026 TX 550h+ entry as of 2026-05-12; source set to model-browse page until EPA publishes, MPG/MPGe values left null.

models.notes: Three powertrain lines: TX 350 ICE (FWD/AWD), TX 500h hybrid AWD, TX 550h+ PHEV AWD. Each has its own base. TX 350 base is FWD; TX 500h base is the F SPORT Performance Premium; TX 550h+ is sole-trim Luxury. Manufacturer-published TX 500h Premium MSRP used as base for that powertrain line.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen UX, refreshed for 2025 with the 2.0L hybrid powertrain replacing the prior 2.0L gas. 2026 is a carryover with minor color/equipment updates and pricing adjustments.
models.model_summary: Lexus's smallest crossover, offered for 2026 only as the UX 300h with a 2.0L Atkinson-cycle hybrid (196 hp combined). FWD standard; AWD optional via an electric on-demand rear motor.

reliability.summary: Long-running platform with a refreshed hybrid powertrain; no 2026 JD Power VDS data published. Owner-reported reliability has been strong.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Note: the Lexus UX was a 2026 JD Power VDS Model Award winner, which strongly correlates with high APEAL ratings. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the 2026 UX 300h's strong fuel economy (43 MPG combined FWD) and refined city manners, while critiquing the small cargo hold (17 cu ft behind 2nd row), slow 0-60 and rather firm ride. The UX is widely expected to be replaced in the US by the LBX or successor.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews positive on efficiency and Lexus reliability; common complaints on cargo size, rear-seat room, and the touch-pad infotainment.

trims.notes: Base trim of the UX 300h (sole-powertrain model; UX is hybrid-only for 2026). Manufacturer source www.lexus.com used; content thin to WebFetch — spec values cross-validated via cars.com during research.
Premium step-up.

notable_other: F SPORT Design styling (mesh grille, bumpers)

changes: F SPORT Design styling (mesh grille, lower bumpers)
F SPORT badging and sport front seats
All Premium content (heated wheel, memory seat, 10-spk)
No chassis changes vs Premium — appearance-only

trims.notes: F SPORT Design — appearance package, no chassis changes.

notable_other: Adaptive Variable Suspension (AVS) and performance damper

changes: Adaptive Variable Suspension (AVS) and performance damper
Adds ventilated seats and leather upholstery

trims.notes: Top UX trim — F SPORT Handling adds AVS and Mark Levinson audio.

models.notes: Single hybrid powertrain (UX 300h); four trim levels. AWD is a $1,570 option on every trim (not separately broken out as a trim). Lexus.com consumer page returned thin content to WebFetch; spec values cross-validated against cars.com during research.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen RZ (introduced 2023 MY) on the e-TNGA platform. 2026 brings a major mid-cycle update: adds RZ 350e (FWD entry trim), increases AWD output (now RZ 450e), and adds RZ 550e F SPORT (402 hp, larger 76.96 kWh battery). All 2026 RZs adopt NACS charging.
models.model_summary: Lexus's all-electric compact crossover, offered for 2026 in three powertrain variants: RZ 350e (single-motor FWD, 221 hp), RZ 450e (dual-motor AWD, 308 hp), and the new RZ 550e F SPORT (dual-motor AWD, 402 hp, larger battery). All adopt the North American Charging System (NACS) port.

reliability.summary: First Lexus BEV; limited long-term reliability data. Carryover 2024-2025 RZ owner reports flag occasional infotainment/connectivity issues but no major drivetrain concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The RZ is low-volume and may not receive a model-specific APEAL ranking. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the 2026 RZ's added power and range improvements, the new 550e F SPORT's 402 hp output, and NACS adoption. Common critiques: range (264-301 mi) still trails newer EV rivals, slow DC fast-charge speeds, and a polarizing 'M Mode' simulated-shift system on the F SPORT.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews limited for the 2026 refresh. Carryover RZ owners praise ride/refinement; complaints on range and DC fast-charge speeds.

notable_other: NACS charging port standard (CCS adapter included)

trims.notes: Base trim of the RZ 350e (single-motor FWD) powertrain line — NEW for 2026. Per EV mirroring rule, MPGe values are mirrored into fuel_economy.city_mpg/highway_mpg/combined_mpg. 301-mi EPA range with standard 18-inch wheels; 20-inch wheels reduce range to ~282 mi (id 50216, separate EPA entry).

changes: EPA range drops to 282 mi (vs 301 mi)
Adds Premium-grade interior trim and badging

trims.notes: Premium FWD step-up of RZ 350e — 20-inch wheels reduce range.

Base trim of the RZ 450e dual-motor AWD line (treated as separate powertrain line from 350e because output and motor configuration differ substantially). Per EV mirroring rule, MPGe values mirrored into fuel_economy fields.
Premium step-up of RZ 450e.

changes: Range drops to 220 mi (staggered 20s + heavier equipment)

trims.notes: Top RZ 450e luxury trim.

powertrain.transmission: single-speed direct drive (with M Mode simulated 8-speed)

notable_other: NEW for 2026: F SPORT EV trim with 402 hp combined
M Mode simulated 8-speed manual via paddles

trims.notes: Sole-trim performance EV (singleton powertrain line — higher-output dual-motor system with a larger 76.96 kWh battery vs the 74.69 kWh on 350e/450e). Per sole-trim atomic rule: is_base_trim:true and delta_from_base:null. New for 2026. Introduces M Mode (paddle-shifted simulated 8-speed) — a Lexus-first feature.

models.notes: Three powertrain lines: RZ 350e (single-motor FWD), RZ 450e (dual-motor AWD), and RZ 550e F SPORT (higher-output dual-motor AWD with larger battery — treated as its own line). All-new 2026 hardware: NACS port standard, RZ 350e FWD is new entry trim, RZ 550e F SPORT is new top performance trim. Multiple EPA IDs per powertrain due to wheel-size variants (18 vs 20).
